issues: The draft describes the Richmond Sockeyes result in isolation, but the OCR shows this is a brief multi-team BCJHL quarter-final roundup paragraph — the Sockeyes result is just one line within it.
The draft says the Sockeyes 'defeated Juan De Fuca 6-1 to win the series 4-2' which is accurate, but omits that the game was played in Victoria.
No headline is present in the OCR — the draft correctly leaves it blank.
No byline is present in the OCR — the draft correctly leaves it blank.
The long description slightly misrepresents the structure: it implies a full article, when in fact the OCR contains only a single paragraph covering multiple BCJHL quarter-final results.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: A brief BCJHL quarter-final roundup notes the Richmond Sockeyes coasted past Juan De Fuca 6-1 in Victoria to win their series 4-2, alongside results for Kelowna, Penticton, Delta, and Nanaimo.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A short BCJHL playoff roundup paragraph reports that the Richmond Sockeyes defeated Juan De Fuca 6-1 in Victoria to win their quarter-final series 4-2 and advance to the next round. The same paragraph notes Delta beat Nanaimo 6-1 to also win 4-2, Kelowna eliminated Penticton to advance to a semi-final against the Vernon Lakers, and that Vernon had swept Merritt in four straight games in their quarter-final.
review.notes: The OCR contains only a single paragraph — a multi-team roundup blurb, not a standalone article. No headline or byline is visible. The Sockeyes content is brief but clearly relevant. Date context (1988-03-03) places this in the 1987-88 BCJHL season, not the 1986-87 championship season.
